statement: User IDs entering this system are UUID v4 strings (36 chars, hyphenated lowercase hex).
breaks_when: An integration introduces a user identifier that is not a UUID v4 — e.g. an enterprise SSO provider that supplies email-as-ID, an imported account batch with integer IDs, or a partner integration using their own ID format.
boundary: Convert at the integration boundary: if an external system provides a non-UUID identifier, mint a new internal UUID for that user and store the external ID separately. Never propagate non-UUID identifiers into the internal user-ID slot.
